subject: Mathematics
title: How RSA Encryption Works
description: A short lesson to help you apply How RSA Encryption Works.

hook.text: Every secure website uses a lock built from prime numbers.

buildup.text: Pick two large primes and multiply them. The result becomes your public key.

discovery.text: Anyone can encrypt a message with your public key. Only you can decrypt it with the primes.

twist.text: Breaking the code means factoring the product back into primes—a task that takes centuries.

climax.text: RSA protects banking, email, and messaging for billions of people worldwide.

punchline.text: Two primes keep your secrets safe.

quiz.question: What makes RSA encryption hard to break?
options: Factoring large numbers is extremely slow
